rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score (2/10) and cite the same substantive disqualifiers: lack of native-level Japanese, missing C/Linux-kernel and embedded networking (L2/L3, MACsec, 802.1X) experience. The differences are reworded phrasing plus a minor discrepancy in the experience tally (15+ vs 10+ years), which reflects ordinary run-to-run variation rather than a penalty tied to the name. No concern, strength, or score shift keys off the change from Bogdan Szabo to James Smith.
